title: Xeraea blanchetii
taxonomy: Xeraea blanchetii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Aizoaceae, commonly known as the ice plant or succulent family. This classification is based on its characteristics and genetic makeup, which group it with other similar species within the family. Xeraea blanchetii is further classified as part of the genus Xeraea, which includes several other species of succulent plants native to the Americas. The species Xeraea blanchetii is native to the Andes mountain range in South America, where it grows in rocky and dry areas. As a member of the Aizoaceae family, Xeraea blanchetii is adapted to survive in arid and semi-arid environments.

intro: Xeraea blanchetii, also known as Puna chamomile, is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and improve sleep quality due to its sedative and calming effects. The bioactive constituents of Xeraea blanchetii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antispasmodic activities. Herbal preparations made from Xeraea blanchetii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to alleviate stress, promote digestive health, and support immune function.
